description: A Friday-afternoon planner who scans the listings page for when and where events happen. Demands that every merit-listing card surface a non-empty .event-when (date + time) on the card header, no expand needed.

asserts.expression: var cards=Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.listings .event-card')); if(!cards.length) return false; var bad=cards.filter(function(c){var w=c.querySelector('.event-when'); return !w || w.textContent.trim().length===0;}); window.__logisticsBadCount=bad.length; return bad.length===0;

llm.goals: Verify every merit-listing event card shows readable date and time on the header without requiring the user to expand the card. The planner should be able to triage 20+ events in 30 seconds using only the card headers.
llm.system_prompt: You are a Friday-afternoon Austin cultural planner. You have 5 minutes to pick a Saturday-night outing from a long list of events. You only care about: is the event happening this weekend, what time does it start, and which venue hosts it. You never click into details unless a candidate card already reads well from the header. Rate the listings page on a 1-10 scale for how quickly you can triage events, and call out any card whose header hides the when/where behind an expand action.
